LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. THE DEMURRER TO THE TWELFTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE EXPIRATION OF THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION ONE THROUGH ELEVEN ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR FAILURE TO ALLEGE FACTS SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION (CCP 430.10(E)). THE CROSS-COMPLAINT, LIBERALLY CONSTRUED, HAS NOT SET FORTH THE ELEMENTS OF A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER ANY THEORY. AS TO THE 1ST AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MARTIN ENG ("MR. ENG") HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT. AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT WAS INVOLVED IN BUSINESS OR THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT CAUSED INJURY TO CONSUMERS. AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH A RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE PARTIES OR TO ESTABLISH THERE ARE UNCERTAIN SUMS RELATING TO DAMAGES. AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THERE WAS AN EXPECTATION OF COMPENSATION. AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH REASONABLE OR FORESEEABLE RELIANCE ON A PROMISE. AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH THAT CROSS-DEFENDANT HAD A LEGAL DUTY TO MR. ENG. AS TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION, CROSS-COMPLAINANT HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH CROSS-DEFENDANT MADE A REPRESENTATION WITH THE INTENT TO HAVE MR. ENG RELY, THAT MR. ENG ACTUALLY RELIED, AND MR. ENG WAS INJURED AS A RESULT OF THAT RELIANCE. AS TO THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH GOODS WERE PURCHASED FROM CROSS-DEFENDANT. (PLEASE SEE THE SCANNED ORDER FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING).

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, AS TO THE FOURTH AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED, WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST, SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. FOR THE FIRST AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, THE CLAIMS ARE PREDICATED ON A CONTRACT BETWEEN PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC. AND WHITE SNOW CORPORATION ("WSC"). MR. ENG IS NOT PERSONALLY A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT AND DOES NOT HAVE STANDING TO SUE ON THIS CONTRACT MERELY BY VIRTUE OF HIS CAPACITY AS AN OWNER OR OFFICER. (HATCHWELL V. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA (1988) 198 CAL.APP.3D 1027, 1034.) FOR THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT ALLEGED ANY FACTS TO SHOW THAT HE, AS OPPOSED TO WSC WAS "A PERSON WHO HAS SUFFERED INJURY IN FACT AND HAS LOST MONEY OR PROPERTY AS A RESULT OF UNFAIR COMPETITION." (BUS. & PROF. CODE 17204.) FOR THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLEADED THAT HE PERSONALLY, AS OPPOSED TO WSC, SUFFERED ANY DAMAGE FROM THE ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION CONCERNING THE PRICING STRUCTURE FOR SALE OF GOODS TO WSC. FOR THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, MR. ENG HAS NOT PLED ANY OF THE ELEMENTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY WITH RESPECT TO HIM PERSONALLY. (CACI NO. 3210.) LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S MOTION FOR ORDER TAXING COSTS AND STRIKING COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT STRIKES ITEM 10 OF THE COSTS MEMORANDUM [PLAINTIFF'S FEE REQUEST OF $39,252.50]. DEFENDANT IS CORRECT THAT A MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES IS NECESSARY. (DEFENDANT'S POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, 2:19-20). CRC 3.1702 PROVIDES THAT A PARTY MUST MAKE A MOTION FOR FEES UNLESS THE PREVAILING PARTY IS ENTITLED TO FIXED FEES PER STATUTE OR CONTRACT. WHERE THE FEES ARE FIXED, THE PREVAILING PARTY CAN LIST THEM IN THE COSTS MEMO. WHERE THE FEES ARE NOT FIXED AND THE COURT MUST MAKE A DETERMINATION, A MOTION IS NECESSARY. CRC 3.1702(E). HERE, PLAINTIFF MISTAKENLY RELIED ON THE COSTS MEMORANDUM PROCEDURE WHEN IT SHOULD HAVE FILED A MOTION FOR FEES. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW ITS REQUEST FOR $39,252.50 IN FEES ARE "FIXED" BY CONTRACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

Notice Of Motion For Order Taxing Costs And Striking Costs
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S Motion For Order Taxing Costs And Striking Costs - The motion is granted in part. The court strikes item 10 of the costs memorandum [plaintiff's fee request of $39,252.50]. Defendant is correct that a motion for attorney's fees is necessary. (Defendant's points and authorities, 2:19-20). CRC 3.1702 provides that a party must make a motion for fees unless the prevailing party is entitled to fixed fees per statute or contract. Where the fees are fixed, the prevailing party can list them in the costs memo. Where the fees are not fixed and the court must make a determination, a motion is necessary. CRC 3.1702(e). Here, plaintiff mistakenly relied on the costs memorandum procedure when it should have filed a motion for fees. Plaintiff fails to explain how its request for $39,252.50 in fees are "fixed" by contract. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj
Off Calendar Oct-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or in the alternative Summary Adjudication - Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Plaintiff's Motion in the Alternative for Summary Adjudication is granted in part and denied in part. The Motion is granted as to the cause of action for breach of contract against Defendant Martin Eng. It is undisputed that 1) there was a contract between White Snow Corporation and Plaintiff; 2) Eng is the owner of Defendant White Snow Corporation; 3) Eng agreed to be held jointly and severally liable for all amounts owed by White Snow in the event of default; 4) White Snow breached the contract; 5) Plaintiff did not breach the contract; and 6) White Snow owes to Plaintiff $35,601.93. (UMF # 1, 2.) Plaintiff is therefore entitled to judgment against Eng. The Motion is denied as to the cause of action for open book account because Plaintiff has failed to produce any evidence of a record constituting an open book. The Motion is also denied as to the cause of action for account stated. Plaintiff has failed to produce evidence that Eng promised to pay the amount stated. Furthermore, the opposition filed by Defendant Eng was untimely filed. However, even if the opposition was considered, the Court would not alter the ruling as Eng failed to raise any triable issues of material fact. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend, as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. Sustained, without leave to amend as to the first, second and third causes of action. For the first and third causes of action, the claims are predicated on a contract between Prince of Peace Enterprises, Inc. and White Snow Corporation ("WSC"). Mr. Eng is not personally a party to the contract and does not have standing to sue on this contract merely by virtue of his capacity as an owner or officer. (Hatchwell v. Blue Shield of California (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 1027, 1034.) For the second cause of action, Mr. Eng has not alleged any facts to show that he, as opposed to WSC was "a person who has suffered injury in fact and has lost money or property as a result of unfair competition." (Bus. & Prof. Code 17204.) For the fourth cause of action, Mr. Eng has not pleaded that he personally, as opposed to WSC, suffered any damage from the alleged misrepresentation concerning the pricing structure for sale of goods to WSC. For the fifth cause of action, Mr. Eng has not pled any of the elements to support a claim for breach of the implied warranty of merchantability with respect to him personally. (CACI no. 3210.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. CROSS DEFENDANT PRINCE OF PEACE ENTERPRISES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The expiration of the statute of limitations is not a cause of action. The demurrer to causes of action one through eleven are sustained with leave to amend for failure to allege facts sufficient to state a cause of action (CCP 430.10(e)). The Cross-Complaint, liberally construed, has not set forth the elements of a cause of action under any theory. As to the 1st and 3rd causes of action, Cross-Complainant Martin Eng ("Mr. Eng") has not plead facts sufficient to establish the existence of a contract. As to the 2nd cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Cross-Defendant was involved in business or that Cross-Defendant caused injury to consumers. As to the 4th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish a relationship between the parties or to establish there are uncertain sums relating to damages. As to the 5th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish there was an expectation of compensation. As to the 6th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish reasonable or foreseeable reliance on a promise. As to the 7th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish that Cross-Defendant had a legal duty to Mr. Eng. As to the 8th cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not plead facts sufficient to establish Cross-Defendant made a representation with the intent to have Mr. Eng rely, that Mr. Eng actually relied, and Mr. Eng was injured as a result of that reliance. As to the 9th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish goods were purchased from Cross-Defendant. As to the 10th cause of action, Cross-Complainant has not plead with sufficient particularity, as a fraud cause of action requires the complaint to "show how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered." (Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73.) As to the 11th cause of action, Mr. Eng has not plead facts sufficient to establish Cross-Defendant intended to inflict emotional distress or that Mr. Eng was present at the time the conduct occurred. Cross-Complainant is granted 20 days leave to amend from notice of entry of order, if he can do so in good faith, to plead facts sufficient to state a cause of action against Cross-Defendant. Order and Judgment signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
